comments.body: If I set the timeout to 0 will it just exit when the connection ends?

@kaysievers

How would this work with socket activation.  Basically I want the podman varlink executable to run for a very short period of time, and then be restarted via socket activation.  Do we risk missing calls?
In an ideal world, we want all requests serviced by a separate Podman process.

> How would this work with socket activation.

Started on-demand, exiting when the specified timeout is reached. The timeout starts counting when there are no active clients connections anymore. When exited, any new request will activate it again. Probably like you expect :)
Excellent @kaysievers.  Now we can it to timeout in a second and not have it running all the time.
Can you also fix the commit message and the github to say 1 second instead of 10 seconds.

:pushpin: Commit bc505f7 has been approved by `baude`
